methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $700M last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 9 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $120M - $6.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

private.name: KoBold Metals
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $2B+

private.name: Electric Hydrogen
private.rationale: Same sector (Climate & Energy), same stage (Series C), post-money $1B+

data.narrative:
Amogy is a Series C climate and energy company founded by Seonghoon Woo, valued at $700M post-money following $300M+ in total fundraising. The valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 9 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage companies—8 private and 1 public (LNZA)—including KoBold Metals ($2.0B Series C), Electric Hydrogen ($1.0B Series C), and Commonwealth Fusion Systems ($5.0B Series B).

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology, using a typical Series C to next-stage multiplier of 1.7x applied to the $700M last post-money valuation, yields an implied value of $1.2B. Peer-median analysis across the 9-company comparable set produced a median valuation of $500M, with a range spanning $120M to $6.0B, reflecting the substantial dispersion in late-stage climate-tech valuations.

These two approaches triangulate to a range of $425M to $1.4B, with a median of $845M. The current $700M valuation sits near the lower quartile of this range. Key uncertainties driving dispersion include the maturity profile of comparable companies—ranging from Series B through Series D—and the nascent, pre-revenue nature typical of frontier-energy ventures. The wide peer range ($120M–$6.0B) underscores the substantial heterogeneity in technology, commercialization timeline, and capital intensity within the Climate & Energy sector.
